meaning: n.(书面)命令,训令,要求,(前国际联盟的)委任托管权vt.委任统治
examples: The bank had no mandate to honour the cheque.
The Belgians took over Rwanda under a League of Nations mandate.
The press secretary told reporters that a majority of one would be a sufficient mandate.
The troops' mandate was to protect observers who would take notes on ceasefire violations.
Ministers also plan to abolish the dual mandate that allows AMs to sit as MPs.
